---
title: "Client Report - Exploring Names"
subtitle: "Unit 1 Task 1"
author: "Nathan McDowell"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
        source: false
        toggle: true
        caption: See code
execute: 
  warning: false
    
---

```{python}
import polars as pl
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
```

```{python}
# Learn morea about Code Cells: https://quarto.org/docs/reference/cells/cells-jupyter.html

# Include and execute your code here
df = pl.read_csv("https://posit.byui.edu/content/0d43d66f-d3b7-4c43-8c36-52e3ed69e7f1/names_year_csv.csv")
df.head()
```

## QUESTION 1

__What was the earliest year that the name ‘Felisha’ was used?__

The earliest use of the name Felisha was in 1964.

```{python}
# Q1
df.filter(pl.col("name") == "Felisha").select(pl.col("year").min())

```


## QUESTION 2

__What year had the most babies named 'David'? How many babies were named 'David' that year?__

The year 1955 had the most babies named David. There were 86389 of them.

```{python}
# Q2
df.filter(pl.col("name") == "David").group_by("year").agg(pl.col("Total").sum().alias("total")).sort("total", descending=True).head(1)

```


## QUESTION 3

__What year did your name hit its peak? How many babies were named your name in that year?__

My name, Nathanael, hit it's peak in 1998. There were 568 babies born with that name in that year.
```{python}
# Q3
df.filter(pl.col("name") == "Nathanael").group_by("year").agg(pl.col("Total").sum().alias("total")).sort("total", descending=True).head(1)

```


## QUESTION 4

__In the most recent year, what was the most common female name in Utah?__ 

Olivia is the most common female name in the most recent year.

```{python}
# Q5
df.filter(pl.col("year") == df["year"].max()).select(["name", "UT"]).sort("UT", descending=True).head(10)

```


---
